context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=13; meal=strawberries with whipped cream; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=no microwave; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or a cold dessert AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front, watching TV on the right, working on a laptop on the left, or social with a person on the left, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to heat fruit-based meals, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, social with a person on the right, or watching TV on the right, THEN 2 min BECAUSE repeated selections for sauced savory meals support 2 min as the user's main reheating preference across multiple contexts. IF food is a warm savory meal with separate components suitable for reheating AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the right or watching TV in front, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected stronger reheating for meals such as chicken nuggets with broccoli and bite-sized pizza with broccoli in those contexts. IF food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or condiment AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves watching TV in front, THEN 1 min can occur as a lighter-reheat option BECAUSE the user previously selected lighter reheating for buffalo chicken bites, wedges, and ranch in that same front-TV context, so front-TV savory meals show item-specific variation rather than a single fixed microwave time. IF food is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND dining context involves working on a laptop on the left, THEN 1 min can occur as a lighter-reheat exception BECAUSE the user previously selected 1 min for the same general context, though 2 min remains better supported overall. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly to avoid blocking a social partner on the right. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed the same partner-aware pattern mirrored to the left side. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly while attending to a front-facing TV. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently preferred to preserve visibility toward a right-side attentional target in those meals. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the new right-TV fruit meal shows that right-side attentional contexts do not always trigger occlusion minimization, and food/context combinations can soften that concern. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted no occlusion constraint in left-side nonsocial attention contexts. Overall, social partner location drives side-specific occlusion minimization more strongly than nonsocial side-attention contexts.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN fast is common but medium can also occur BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored quicker interaction, while later front-TV evidence shows the user does not always want maximum speed in that context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN fast BECAUSE the user preferred a quicker pace while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a less hurried pace for a fruit meal in the same work context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right OR social with a person on the right OR social with a person on the left OR watching TV on the right OR watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user retained a moderate pace in those contexts rather than accelerating. No broader affect-dependent variation observed yet.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user has consistently selected this option across meals, favoring bite acquisition reliability. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN no BECAUSE the user has consistently skipped explicit web confirmation of bite acquisition, indicating a stable preference to reduce interaction overhead. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop on the left, working on a laptop on the right, or social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user usually chose direct delivery when attention was occupied or in the observed social-left setting, indicating that reduced-effort transfer often overrides their physical ability to lean forward. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly in that social setting, indicating a stable preference for the less intrusive transfer style there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals supported the less intrusive, user-reachable style there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the new right-TV strawberries meal shows that in the same environmental context the user may switch to lower-effort direct delivery for lighter cold foods. Across observed contexts, the user is physically capable of outside-mouth transfer but often prefers inside-mouth transfer when convenience or reduced effort appears more salient.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right or watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected an outside-mouth distance that balances comfort and reachability in those settings. IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer, THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter does not apply when the robot delivers food directly into the mouth.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue was previously common but LED also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals initially showed a preference to remove readiness notifications, while later evidence shows the user may retain a lightweight visual cue rather than fully suppressing prompts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed readiness notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an explicit auditory cue for the fruit meal, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly favor cue suppression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right OR social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal readiness cue in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left OR watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN LED BECAUSE the user retained a lightweight visual cue in those contexts rather than suppressing cues or requiring multimodal prompting. The observed social contexts differ: social-right favors lighter visual cueing, while social-left favored stronger multimodal cueing.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly, consistent with avoiding ambiguity from conversational mouth movements in social settings. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed the user accepted a lower-effort initiation method there rather than maintaining explicit button control. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-TV savory context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN button BECAUSE the new right-TV fruit meal shows the user can shift back to explicit initiation in the same environmental context, so food type matters there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user retained explicit initiation in that side-TV context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored fully automatic initiation, while later front-TV evidence shows the user can shift back to explicit control in the same context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user shifted to a low-effort but still user-driven initiation method. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred low-effort embodied initiation rather than button press or full automation. Across observed cases, social settings do not uniformly force explicit control: social-right favors button, while social-left favored open mouth.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for drink initiation in those contexts, and the new right-TV meal reinforces that pattern. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation preference there. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals had shown a preference for automatic drink initiation, while later evidence demonstrates that explicit control can still be preferred there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit initiation for drinking in the observed left-work meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, or watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN button BECAUSE the user chose explicit signaling for mouth-wiping initiation in those contexts, and the new right-TV meal reinforces that pattern. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation preference there. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue was previously common but button also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals had shown a preference for automatic wiping initiation, while later evidence demonstrates that explicit control can still be preferred there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the user selected explicit initiation for wiping in the observed left-work meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected a lower-effort but still user-driven initiation method in that right-work context.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no cue was previously common but LED also occurs BECAUSE repeated front-TV meals initially showed a preference to suppress completion notifications, while later evidence shows the user may instead keep a lightweight visual completion cue. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no cue BECAUSE the user suppressed completion notifications while engaged in a concurrent task. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected an auditory completion cue for the fruit meal, showing that work contexts do not uniformly favor cue suppression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right OR social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected the most explicit multimodal completion cue in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the right OR involves watching TV on the left OR watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN LED BECAUSE the user retained the default visual completion cue in those contexts. The observed social contexts differ: social-right favors lighter visual cueing, while social-left favored stronger multimodal cueing.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently preferred reliable automatic detection for bite completion rather than requiring extra manual input across all observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the accumulated evidence shows a repeated preference for explicit completion signaling for drinking in many contexts, and the new right-TV meal further strengthens that pattern. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed the user accepted automatic progression there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is strongly occupied in that context, reduced interaction burden can override explicit control.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN button BECAUSE the accumulated evidence shows a repeated preference for explicit completion signaling for mouth wiping in many contexts, and the new right-TV meal further strengthens that pattern. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed social-left meal showed the user accepted automatic progression there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE prior right-work evidence indicates that when attention is occupied, the user can prefer lower-effort automatic progression.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN yes BECAUSE this has been selected repeatedly, indicating a stable preference to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in that social setting even at the cost of longer meal time. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no BECAUSE the observed social-left meal prioritized faster, less interrupted interaction rather than retracting. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting in a right-work context, consistent with protecting visibility toward a right-side task. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals supported prioritizing faster, less interrupted interaction there. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the new right-TV strawberries meal shows that in the same environmental context the user may accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction for lighter cold foods. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no was previously common but yes also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals favored not retracting, while later front-TV evidence shows the user may accept longer interaction to reduce obstruction in that same context. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-TV meals show the user prefers not to retract. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritized faster, less interrupted interaction while working. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retracting for the fruit meal, indicating that work contexts do not uniformly suppress retraction and that meal type can modulate willingness to accept longer interaction for less obstruction.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a separate dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred some dipping while limiting messiness in a social context. IF food includes a topping or dippable accompaniment associated with the bite AND dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like, THEN less BECAUSE the observed strawberries with whipped cream meal showed the user again preferred some but not maximal dipping/topping in a social setting. IF food has no dippable item or sauce, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree with sauce already incorporated rather than a separate dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user does not treat incorporated sauce as a cue to dip. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, watching TV on the left, working on a laptop on the left, or working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is food with sauce already incorporated or with a separate condiment/dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated selections show the user generally avoids dipping outside social contexts, even when ranch, ketchup, whipped cream, or other condiments/toppings are available. Overall, observed social settings favor less rather than none when a dip or topping is available, whereas nonsocial contexts favor not dipping.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that side-TV context, suggesting a preference for ample intervention time when attention is split laterally. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meals support a moderate wait rather than maximal delay. IF dining context involves watching TV on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a cold fruit/dessert meal, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the new right-TV strawberries meal shows the user may want much more intervention time for lighter cold foods in that same context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 10 sec was previously common but 1000 sec also occurs BECAUSE earlier front-TV meals showed a preference for rapid automatic progression, while later front-TV evidence shows the user can instead want very long intervention time in that same context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory entree, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very short wait while multitasking. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait for the fruit meal, showing that in work contexts food type can shift the user between rapid automation and preserving ample time to intervene. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected a short wait in that right-work context, favoring quick automatic progression. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any food, THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in that social setting, balancing explicit control with enough time to respond without making the interaction excessively slow. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is unspecified/neutral-like AND food_condition is any observed food, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed social-left meal favored quick automatic progression rather than preserving long intervention time. Across social contexts, partner side matters: social-right supports a moderate wait, while social-left supported a short wait.
